checks.a_analysis: The output text accurately reflects the meeting minutes without adding fabricated prose or sections. Duplicate tables (e.g., the roll call vote table appearing on both Page 4 and Page 6) are present in the source PDF and are correctly preserved in the output.

Comparing the phone numbers on page 3 of the source PDF with the output, a digit error was found in one number. The source number '910-564-4194' was converted to '910-564-2194'. This is a critical accuracy failure.
All seven pages of the source document are represented in the output JSON. Each page's content, including tables and committee reports, has a corresponding section or element in the output.

The row counts for the large attendance and roll call tables were verified against the source. All material rows, including the list of 32 guests and various board members, are accounted for in the output.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A significant omission was identified in the Consent Agenda section on Page 4. The sub-bullets detailing item 5f ('i. Monthly Reports per PSP' and 'ii. Mitchell County Grant Extension Request') are entirely missing from the output list.
checks.b_evidence_source: i. Monthly Reports per PSP ii. Mitchell County Grant Extension Request

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are transcription errors in the names listed in the tables that alter meaning. Specifically, 'Buddy Martinette' is misspelled as 'Buddy Marinette' in the attendance table, and 'Earl Struble' is misspelled as 'Earl Sturble' in the first roll call vote table.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 2 reading_order 9 row 10; Section 4 reading_order 35 row 15

checks.a_analysis: The output contains two instances of the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table (index 35 and index 49). This is a faithful representation of the source document, which contains the exact same table embedded on Page 4 and again on Page 6.

The heading hierarchy skips level 2. The document title at index 1 is H1. The next heading at index 36 ('6. Executive Director Report') is assigned H3, skipping H2. This is a violation of semantic heading structure.

Multiple tables exhibit structural corruption. 1) In the 'ROLL CALL' table (index 9), the header row contains 4 columns, but several data rows (e.g., Row 3, Eric Boyette) contain only 3 cells. This causes data to shift; for example, Eric Boyette's vote mark for 'VIA MS TEAMS' (column 3 in the source) is placed in cell 2 (column 2 'IN PERSON') in the output. 2) The 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table at index 35 is missing its header row entirely ('NC 911 BOARD MEMBERS', etc.), starting directly with data. 3) The 'GUESTS' table at index 12 has inconsistent column counts, with Row 1 having 2 cells while Row 7 has 3 cells.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the 'ROLL CALL' table on Page 2, Eric Boyette has an 'X' in the third column ('VIA MS TEAMS/PHONE').
checks.b_evidence_output: In index 9, Row 3 has 3 cells total. Cell 2 is 'X'. Cells in row 1 define col 2 as 'IN PERSON' and col 3 as 'VIA MS TEAMS'.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the visual flow of the PDF pages correctly, including titles, tables, and paragraphs.

The 'Consent Agenda' section on Page 4 contains fund accounts (c, d, e) with nested lists of balances and disbursements. The output (index 24-32) loses this list structure, rendering the items as a flat sequence of paragraphs.
checks.b_evidence_source: c) CMRS Fund/Account\nApril 2020 Month-End Balance $7,769,914\nApril 2020 Disbursement $0
checks.b_evidence_output: Three separate paragraph elements (index 24, 25, 26) with no list association.

checks.a_analysis: There are no links in the source document, and none were incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-column attendance tables, the structured roll call tables, and the numbered list of meeting sections are all presented in the same order and layout style as the original document. Content is not rearranged in a way that confuses meaning; it follows a logical single-column flow that mirrors the source's intent.

All tables in the document, including the attendance grids and the roll call vote tables, are correctly rendered in HTML. Borders are present where appropriate, columns are correctly aligned, and headers are visually distinct (often using bold text or background shading, as seen in the 'ROLL CALL' tables). The data is clearly readable and the table structure matches the source PDF.

The meaningful images in the source PDF, specifically the 'NC DIT' logos within the roll call tables, are correctly included and rendered in the HTML output. The red diagonal watermark ('APPROVED BY NC911 BOARD...') present in the PDF is correctly omitted from the HTML as it is a decorative/status indicator, which aligns with accessibility standards for clean conversion.

The content in the HTML matches the source PDF without unintentional duplication. While some tables (like the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table) and lists (Guest names) appear twice in the HTML, they are also present twice in the source PDF itself (e.g., the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table appears on both page 4 and page 6 of the original document). The HTML correctly reflects this redundancy from the source material. Section headings appearing in the Table of Contents and then again in the body is standard accessible design and not a defect.
